prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG planetary atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Warhammer 40,000 / Dark Imperium inspired grimdark far-future world map, diverse archipelago terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye imperial cartographic scan of a vast toxic ocean world filled with many island groups, microcontinents, orbital platforms, and industrial war zones.
Subject: diverse archipelago layout with uneven island distribution across a broad toxic ocean: several large hive-islands, microcontinents, tiny islets, curved island arcs, lagoons, reef belts, narrow straits, remote atolls, volcanic islands, fragmented industrial coasts, fortress islands, refinery atolls, voidport platforms, underhive island-sprawls, ash waste islands, irradiated tundra islands, and ruined battle-zone islands. Include multiple climates and industrial biomes across the islands.
Style/medium: imperial sector star map fused with planetary atlas, gothic architecture aesthetic, mechanicus technical overlay, grimdark science-fantasy cartography, highly detailed illustrated RPG sourcebook map.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, no single dominant mainland, island groups sweep in organic arcs and clusters, full archipelago visible with toxic ocean border and subtle non-text cogitator grid.
Lighting/mood: brutal grimdark far future, industrial hellscape, toxic ochre atmosphere, holy war and Inquisition dread, dangerous sea lanes and orbital war logistics.
Color palette: blackened parchment, toxic ochre, rust red, oil-slick teal and black ocean, ash grey, dirty brass, plasma glow accents.
Materials/textures: weathered vellum, dark metal frame, etched lines, auspex scan noise, engraved coastlines, gothic technical detail.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Avoid grid-like spacing and avoid identical island shapes.
